query:
OQ1 — Edge direction: unidirectional, checking both directions is 
  required (not just safe).                        
                                                                    
  The code at line 509–523 writes (source_id, neighbor_id,          
  'Contradicts') only — one direction, always from the new entry    
  toward its neighbor. There is no reverse write. This means a      
  contradiction between entries A and B will have an edge in        
  whichever direction happened to be the "new entry" at detection   
  time. The suppress_contradicts function must check both A→B and
  B→A to be correct, not just safe. Confirm this in the SCOPE.md —
  "both directions for safety" should read "both directions
  required; edges are unidirectional at write time."

  OQ2 — context_lookup: correct, out of scope.

  Suppression applies to ranked lists where multiple candidates     
  compete. context_lookup is a deterministic fetch by ID — the agent
   already chose the entry. No collision to suppress. If            
  contradiction warnings on single-entry fetch are ever wanted,   
  that's a separate proactive conflict notification feature.

  OQ3 — Audit log enrichment: defer.

  The eval gate for #395 is zero-regression on scenarios with no    
  Contradicts edges — suppression is a no-op in current test data.
  Audit data would show nothing useful until #412 ships real edges. 
  Add it as a follow-up comment on #395 for post-#412 enrichment, 
  same pattern as the EDGE_SOURCE_NLI constant migration on #412.

entries.title: ADR-003: Bidirectional Contradicts Query Required for Correctness (col-030)
ADR-002: edges_of_type as Sole Traversal Boundary for suppress_contradicts
ADR-003 crt-037: Directional Dedup for query_existing_informs_pairs
Helper extraction pattern for infallible bidirectional tick writes
ADR-004 col-028: confirmed_entries Trigger Uses Request-Side Cardinality for context_lookup
